channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: SEBI Approves 3 IPOs as Indian Markets Tumble
item.description: India's market regulator SEBI has approved IPOs for travel tech firm Travelstack Tech, edtech provider Learnfluence Education, and tea chain Tea Post. This comes as Indian stock markets grapple with pressure from West Asian geopolitical tensions, causing significant foreign investor outflows and a broad sell-off. The companies' move signals focused growth plans despite these challenging conditions.

item.title: Intellect Design Arena Loses Top Executives Amid Simplification Push
item.description: Intellect Design Arena will lose two senior leaders, John Owen (President, Western Hemisphere) and Kannan Ramasamy (Chief Partner Officer), on March 31, 2026. The company says the departures are part of a plan to streamline management and reduce organizational layers.

item.title: Accedere Limited Pivots to AI Compliance, Offers 1:10 Bonus Shares
item.description: Accedere Limited is undergoing a significant strategic transformation, evolving into an AI-driven cyber compliance firm. The company highlighted its subsidiary, Freebird Aerospace, and its patented AI technology, including 'Flying Sensors', as a core asset driving this shift. A 1:10 bonus issue was also announced for shareholders, signaling confidence in future growth.

item.title: SEBI Fines Anand Rathi ₹10 Lakh for Cyber Security Failures
item.description: SEBI has fined Anand Rathi Share and Stock Brokers ₹10 lakh for non-compliance with cybersecurity and cyber resilience rules, along with stockbroker regulations. The violations were found during an inspection from April 2023 to August 2024. The penalty signals SEBI's ongoing focus on data protection and operational compliance for financial firms.

item.title: Geopolitical Fears Drive Market Plunge: Value Stocks Emerge
item.description: Indian markets experienced a sharp sell-off, with numerous BSE 500 stocks hitting 52-week lows, driven by escalating geopolitical tensions and volatile oil prices. Foreign institutional investors continued their selling spree, while the rupee weakened significantly. Despite broad-based declines, analysts identify opportunities in fundamentally strong companies trading at attractive valuations, advising strategic portfolio rebalancing.

item.title: India's Auto and Chemical Firms Face Supply Chain Crisis
item.description: India's auto parts and specialty chemicals makers are struggling with major supply chain issues and higher costs. Gas shortages, soaring petrochemical prices, and global shipping problems are hurting production and exports. Despite some optimistic growth outlooks, especially for specialty chemicals and EV parts, companies must focus on diversifying, building resilience, and managing risks in a volatile global market.

item.title: India SIP Growth Steady Yearly, Month-Over-Month Dip Shows Caution
item.description: Systematic Investment Plan (SIP) contributions in February 2026 rose 14.79% year-over-year to ₹29,845 crore, though they dipped 3.73% from January. Active SIP accounts grew to 9.44 crore. Assets under management (AUM) saw marginal moderation, attributed to mark-to-market corrections, not a loss of investor interest. This sustained inflow highlights disciplined long-term saving behavior among Indian households, even as broader market corrections and global uncertainties prompt a more cautious retail investor stance.

item.title: REC Declares 32% Dividend of ₹3.20, Record March 20
item.description: REC Limited's Board approved a 32% interim dividend for FY25-26, paying ₹3.20 per share. Shareholders on record March 20, 2026, will get the payout by April 14, 2026. The move signals REC's commitment to returns, but investors must manage tax compliance.

item.title: Thangamayil Jewellery Promoter Buys Shares, Stake Edges Up
item.description: Promoter NB Kumar of Thangamayil Jewellery Limited purchased 2,650 equity shares on March 12, 2026, via the open market. This transaction marginally increased his stake to 17.5071%. The move reflects continued promoter confidence, though the direct business impact of this small acquisition is minimal.

item.title: Alisha Gandhi Buys Relic Technologies Shares, Lifts Stake to 44.80%
item.description: Promoter Alisha Kunal Gandhi has acquired 1,400 equity shares of Relic Technologies Limited. This acquisition on March 12, 2026, marginally increased her voting rights holding from 44.78% to 44.80%. The total equity share capital remains Rs. 5,59,11,640. The move signals a slight increase in promoter confidence.

item.title: Ola Electric Seeks ₹2,000 Cr Funding for Battery Unit Amid Sales Dip
item.description: Ola Electric is seeking up to ₹2,000 crore for its battery subsidiary, Ola Cell Technologies (OCT). The funds will boost gigafactory expansion and innovation as the company faces slower EV sales and competition from Ather, TVS, and Bajaj.

item.title: Gorani Industries: Nakul Gorani's Stake Rises to 6.21% From Gifted Shares
item.description: Nakul Gorani boosted his direct stake in Gorani Industries Limited to 6.21% by receiving 110,000 shares as a gift from Mrs. Shanta Devi Gorani. The March 16, 2026, off-market transaction consolidates promoter shareholding, directly increasing Nakul Gorani's ownership.

item.title: Transpek Industry Files Promoter Status Change to Public Category with BSE
item.description: Transpek Industry Limited has officially notified the BSE about the reclassification of promoter Malti D. Bhatia to the 'Public category'. This procedural step follows the company's Board approval on February 4, 2026, and subsequent application to the stock exchange, adhering to SEBI regulations for status changes. The applicant holds a minor stake, satisfying the necessary criteria.

item.title: Bandhan Bank Denies Stake Sale Rumours After 12% Stock Drop March 16
item.description: Bandhan Bank has officially denied any knowledge of potential promoter stake sales or IPO plans, attributing a sharp 12.22% stock price fall on March 16, 2026, to market conditions. The bank stated it has not withheld material information and faces no material impact from the unconfirmed news, reinforcing its stance following similar clarifications earlier in March. This denial aims to quell investor concerns amidst market volatility.

item.title: Allcargo Terminals: February Volumes Climb 8% YoY, Daily Throughput Stable
item.description: Allcargo Terminals saw its February 2026 container volumes rise 8% year-on-year to 57.6 thousand TEUs. Although total volumes dipped slightly from January due to fewer operating days, the average daily throughput remained stable, showing consistent operations.

item.title: Geet Gorani Increases Gorani Industries Stake to 6.21%
item.description: Geet Gorani acquired 1,45,362 shares (2.71%) of Gorani Industries from Mrs. Shanta Devi Gorani via an off-market gift. This boosts his total ownership to 6.21%, reinforcing promoter alignment within the company.

item.title: Japan Taps Oil Reserves Amid Price Shock; Mideast Tensions Escalate
item.description: Tokyo is tapping its private oil reserves to ease supply disruptions from the Middle East conflict, joining a global effort coordinated by the IEA. This offers temporary relief as Brent crude stays around $105 a barrel, showing ongoing geopolitical risks. Japan, importing over 99% of its oil, faces major economic impact from high energy costs and a weak security alliance.

item.title: Banks' Old Tech Pushes Clients to Faster Rivals
item.description: Corporate and investment banks (CIBs) are losing clients to non-bank financial institutions (NBFIs) that offer faster, more transparent services. CIBs face challenges with outdated systems, poor integration, and slow AI adoption, dedicating only 29% of IT budgets to transformative tech. This has resulted in slower revenue growth and a significant client trust gap on AI innovation, pushing clients toward NBFIs for solutions.

item.title: Looks Health Shareholders Approve Promoter Shift, OK Gujarat Office Move
item.description: Looks Health Services Limited shareholders overwhelmingly approved key resolutions via postal ballot. The company will reclassify Mr. Pritesh Doshi and Maxgainz Finserve Private Limited to the public category, and shift its registered office from Maharashtra to Gujarat. All resolutions passed with a near-unanimous 99.89% vote in favour.

item.title: India-US Trade Pact Hits Snag Over US Tariff Law Uncertainty
item.description: The India-US trade deal, set to lower US tariffs to 18% by February 2026, faces delays. A US Supreme Court ruling against broad tariffs, combined with Middle East conflict disrupting energy supplies, has created significant uncertainty. India's energy security, including its reliance on Russian oil, is now a critical factor in finalizing the pact.

item.title: Markolines Eyes FY26 Revenue of ₹375-400 Cr, Projects 40-50% Growth for FY27
item.description: Markolines Pavement Technologies targets ₹375-400 crore revenue for FY26, projecting 40-50% growth next year driven by a ₹695 crore order book. The company is merging Markolines Infra and expanding into tunneling, aiming for ₹1,000 crore revenue in 3-4 years. Investors watch margin stability amid crude price risks and monsoon seasonality.

item.title: India's Trade Deficit Widens Sharply on Import Surge, Mideast Tensions
item.description: India's trade deficit grew to $3.96 billion in February 2026, up from $2.72 billion a year earlier. This widening was driven by a significant 21.61% jump in imports to $80.09 billion, which overshadowed an 11.04% rise in exports to $76.13 billion. Merchandise trade faced particular pressure, with imports rising sharply and exports falling slightly, pushing the goods deficit to $27.1 billion. Geopolitical tensions in West Asia are also increasing logistical costs and threatening trade flows.

item.title: Filmcity Media Plans ₹1.9 Cr Share Sale to Fund Pivot
item.description: Filmcity Media will raise ₹1.9 crore by issuing up to 1.90 crore shares at ₹1 each. The shares will go to PMC Fincorp (promoter group) and two other investors. This funding supports the company's strategic shift into real estate and financial services, following recent leadership changes.

item.title: UCAL Ownership in US Unit UHI Drops to 10% After AscentX Deal
item.description: UCAL Limited announced that its US subsidiary, Ucal Holdings Inc. (UHI), will issue 9000 equity shares to AscentX Inc., USA. This move will dilute UCAL's ownership from 100% to 10%, effective March 15, 2026. As a result, UHI will no longer be wholly owned, affecting UCAL's financial reporting and control.

item.title: Mayur Uniquoters Promoter Adds Shares, Lifts Stake to 58.62%
item.description: Kiran Poddar, a promoter of Mayur Uniquoters, acquired 13,600 equity shares on March 13, 2026. This minor open market purchase nudged the promoter group's total shareholding slightly higher to 58.62%. The move signals continued confidence from the promoter family in the company's prospects.

item.title: Afcons Infrastructure Redeems ₹55 Cr Commercial Paper on Due Date
item.description: Afcons Infrastructure Limited has confirmed the redemption of its Commercial Paper (CP) worth ₹55 crore, maturing on March 16, 2026. This fulfillment of its payment obligation underscores the company's commitment to financial discipline. The company operates in the complex EPC sector, navigating ongoing project execution and client payment dynamics.

item.title: Baid Finserv Promoters Boost Stake to 47.39% After Warrant Conversion
item.description: Baid Finserv Limited's promoter group has significantly increased its stake to 47.39% by converting 48,02,732 warrants into equity shares at Rs. 2 per share. This conversion boosts the company's total equity capital and signals strong promoter confidence, following a recent rights issue.

item.title: CM Airtime Acquires 5,000 Chambal Fertilisers Shares, Holding Tops 2 Million
item.description: CM Airtime Promotion LLP acquired 5,000 shares of Chambal Fertilisers and Chemicals Ltd., boosting its total holding to 2,017,552 shares. The acquisition complies with SEBI's substantial acquisition disclosure rules, indicating continued investor interest.

item.title: Intellect Design Arena, Fintel PLC Launch UK AI Finance Advisory JV
item.description: Intellect Design Arena is teaming up with UK's Fintel PLC for a 50:50 joint venture focused on an AI-driven financial advisory platform for the UK market. This expansion aims to tap into growing AI use in finance, pending regulatory approval.

item.title: Bajaj Healthcare Promoter Anil Jain Buys Shares, Lifts Stake to 6.08%
item.description: Anil Champalal Jain, part of Bajaj Healthcare Limited's promoter group, bought 3,000 equity shares on March 13, 2026. This move, along with parties acting in concert with him, raises his total stake to 14,66,775 shares, representing 6.08% of the company's total voting capital. The disclosure was filed on March 16, 2026.

item.title: India's $110 Oil Shock: Economy Strained, Energy Firms Divided
item.description: Escalating Middle East conflict has pushed Brent crude oil futures above $100 per barrel, nearing $106, raising significant concerns for India's economy. Analysts warn that prices sustained above $110 could force unavoidable fuel price hikes, straining government finances and consumer spending. This situation creates a sharp divergence in India's energy sector, with standalone refiners set to gain from higher margins while Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) confront severe margin compression.

item.title: India's Trade Deficit Widens on Import Surge, Pressuring Rupee
item.description: India's overall trade deficit widened to $3.96 billion in February 2026, a significant reversal from the previous year's surplus. Merchandise imports surged by 24% to $63.71 billion, driven by substantial increases in gold and silver purchases, while merchandise exports saw a marginal dip. Though services exports showed robust growth, the rising import bill, especially for precious metals, is pressuring foreign exchange reserves and raising concerns about currency stability and inflation. Economists forecast a widening current account deficit and a weaker rupee for 2026, alongside projected inflation of around 4.2%.

item.title: Supreme Court Questions SEBI Probe After ₹5,100 Crore Sandesara Settlement
item.description: India's Supreme Court has questioned the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) about its ongoing investigation into the Sandesara brothers. The SC suggested SEBI might be blocking the court's earlier order. This follows the promoters' reported ₹5,100 crore payment to settle all cases, as directed by an order. The court's query raises questions about how regulators and courts handle final settlements.

item.title: Bajel Projects Lands ₹50-100 Cr EPC Order from Tata Power
item.description: Bajel Projects Limited has secured a ₹50 Cr to ₹100 Cr EPC order from Tata Power to construct a 220 kV transmission line using Monopoles. The project is set to be completed within 10 months, adding to the company's order book and visibility in the power transmission sector.

item.title: CMPDI IPO: Mining Consultancy Giant Debuts Amid OFS, Client Risk
item.description: Central Mine Planning & Design Institute (CMPDI) launches its IPO with a price band of ₹163-172, seeking to raise ₹1,842.12 crore via an Offer for Sale. The company, holding a 61% market share in India's mining consultancy sector, shows robust financials with FY25 revenue at ₹2,177 crore and PAT at ₹667 crore. Strong grey market premiums suggest potential listing gains. However, the 100% OFS structure means no new capital for growth, and its deep reliance on Coal India presents strategic risks in a shifting energy environment.

item.title: India Motor Insurance: Inefficiencies Fuel Growth to ₹1.7 Trillion
item.description: India's motor insurance sector is characterized by widespread systemic inefficiencies, including mis-selling, opaque pricing, and a significant gap between perceived and actual coverage. Despite these challenges, the market is poised for substantial growth, projected to reach ₹1.7 trillion by 2029. This complex environment, driven by a large and growing vehicle fleet, presents a considerable opportunity for insurers offering transparency, fair pricing, and enhanced customer value. Recent regulatory reforms and technological advancements are beginning to address some long-standing issues, potentially paving the way for more customer-centric products.

item.title: India's Nuclear Plans: Private Insurance Excludes War, Catastrophic Risks
item.description: Standard health insurance in India, following IRDAI rules, excludes war and nuclear incidents. This means claims from declared or undeclared wars, invasions, or injuries from nuclear, chemical, or biological weapons, and radioactive contamination, will be denied by private insurers. Experts cite the uninsurable scale of potential casualties as the main reason, viewing such risks as a sovereign rather than commercial duty. While government schemes and specific laws like the new SHANTI Act, 2025, address nuclear liability, private policyholders lack protection against these extreme events.

item.title: India Trade Gap Widens Annually Even as Monthly Deficit Narrows
item.description: India's merchandise trade deficit narrowed to $27.1 billion in February from $34.68 billion in January, aided by moderating imports and slight export growth. However, this figure represents a significant year-on-year increase from the $14.42 billion deficit recorded in February 2025. Escalating geopolitical tensions in West Asia and rising commodity import costs are creating substantial challenges, raising concerns about future trade balances and currency stability. Analysts project continued vulnerability of its foreign trade.

item.title: PB Fintech's PB Health Faces Conflict: Profit vs. Patient Care
item.description: Parliamentarians are questioning PB Fintech's PB Health venture, an integrated model combining hospitals and insurance. Critics worry that focusing on profit could harm patient care, similar to issues seen with US HMOs. While PB Fintech shows strong financial growth, its high stock valuation and move to a more asset-heavy business model face significant challenges.

item.title: GSP Crop Science IPO: QIBs Invest, Retailers Hesitate as Debt Focus Dominates
item.description: GSP Crop Science's IPO opened Tuesday, reaching 20% subscription by midday. Qualified Institutional Buyers (QIBs) led demand, buying 58% of their allocation. Retail (6%) and NII (3%) interest was notably lower. The company plans to use the Rs 170 crore raised mainly to repay debt, aiming for a stronger balance sheet. GSP Crop Science, an agrochemical firm with 39 years of experience, is navigating a market where rivals trade at higher valuations, prompting questions about its growth versus stability path.
